attempted_route: PDB chain-A ATOM parsing -> local window (±7 residues one-hot, 15*20=300 dims) + physicochemical diff (MUT-WT, 4 dims) + WT/MUT one-hot (40 dims) + Tmexp[wt] + pos_norm + Nres (347 total) -> sklearn.ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor(n_estimators=500, max_depth=4, lr=0.05, subsample=0.8)
observation: Script exited 0, saved 147 predictions (mean=-3.65, std=3.13); eval reported MAE=67.61, virtually identical to round-1 MAE=67.16, indicating the local window one-hot approach failed as completely as global composition.
rationale: 1D local sequence window encodes sequence identity of neighbors but not 3D structural contacts. deltaTm is driven by H-bond networks and packing geometry; one-hot window over ±7 residues in primary sequence is not a valid proxy for local 3D environment and cannot learn the relevant signal regardless of GBR capacity.
recommended_alternative: Use sklearn.neighbors.KNeighborsRegressor or ridge regression on BLOSUM62-derived substitution scores (wt_aa, mut_aa pair lookup via Bio.Align.substitution_matrices.load('BLOSUM62')) combined with Tmexp[wt] and Nres. BLOSUM62 encodes evolutionary accepted substitution patterns which correlate with thermodynamic stability better than one-hot encoding.
